title: RNAWorld
alt: Disney lore: Canonically, because of how Elsa's abiogenesis powers work, Olaf is an RNA-only organism.
transcript:
[Cueball, Ponytail, Jill, and another child with a dark hair (wearing Mickey/Minnie ears) are walking through an amusement park. Cueball has a water bottle and a backpack, Ponytail is looking at a map or a brochure with a helix structure shown on one page, Jill is pointing forward while holding a small stuffed toy resembling , and the other child has a popsicle. Cueballs, Megans, and Hairys can be seen in the background in gray. There are also a drop tower, a roller coaster, a shop, and a hot air balloon in the background.]
Ponytail: Okay, kids, now that Ariel is done collecting nucleotides for Ratatouille’s primordial soup, let’s go watch Elsa initiate runaway ribozyme synthesis!

[Caption below the panel:]
Disney’s RNAWorld
explanation:
This comic conflates  and . Disney World is one of a franchise of theme parks with attractions based on various  movies, while the  is a proposed origin of life, in which RNA acts both as the genetic material and 

 is the titular character from . In the film she likes to collect human artifacts; the comic replaces this with collecting , the basic building blocks of  and .

 is a film about a French rat named Remy (not "Ratatouille"; see ) who dreams of becoming a gourmet chef. The comic conflates the soup that a chef might create for patrons to eat with "", the environment that's believed to have existed on the early Earth when the processes of life began.

 is one of the lead characters from the  movies. In the film she has the magical ability to control ice and snow, and she used this power to make the snowman  come to life. The comic equates this with the original , or life from non-life, through  synthesis. Ribozymes are RNA molecules that, similarly to enzymes made of protein, catalyze biochemical reactions, such as the splicing of RNA during gene expression.

The title text continues this by saying that since Elsa's ability is based on ribozymes, Olaf's machinery of life must be based only on RNA, not DNA. This fits in with the theme of RNA World. Olaf generally appears to be (by mass) mostly just snow but, in common with various ideas about  (and the role played by them in 'seeding' the young Earth with organic molecules), might well be thoroughly imbued with carbon-rich compounds other than those inherent in his carrot nose, coal buttons, and basic twig/stick elements. "Canonically" refers to  (in this case Disney fiction), "the body of works taking place in a particular fictional world that are widely considered to be official or authoritative." RNA has also been mentioned in 2425: mRNA Vaccine and 3056: RNA.
